data.title: Anbio Biotechnology prices $8M IPO at $5 per share
data.description:
Global diagnostic company, Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN) priced its initial public offering of 1.6M shares at $5.00 per share.

The Class A ordinary shares have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq and are expected to commence trading on February 19, 2025 under the ticker symbol “NNNN.”

The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of $8M.

The offering is expected to close on or about February 20, 2025.

Proceeds from the offering will be used for expansion of sales and distribution network in the strategically selected markets, research and development, and working capital and general corporate matters.

Anbio Biotechnology is dedicated to the advancement of medical technology and the provision of in vitro diagnostics products.

data.title: Japan reports larger-than-expected trade deficit for Jan, imports outpaces exports
data.description:
Japan’s trade deficit increased significantly to JPY 2,758.78 billion in January 2025 from JPY 1,766.54 billion in the same month a year earlier, exceeding market consensus for a gap of JPY 2,100 billion, as imports grew much faster than exports.

Exports from Japan rose by 7.2% y/y to JPY 7,863.75 billion in January 2025, picking up from a 2.8% growth in December while pointing to the fourth month of gain.

Imports to Japan increased by 16.7% y/y to a 26-month high of JPY 10,622.52 billion in January 2025, sharply picking up from a 1.7% growth in December and easily exceeding forecasts of 9.7%. It was the strongest rise in purchases since January 2023, with imports of mineral fuels rising 4.0%

Separate data showed, core machinery orders in Japan, a crucial gauge of capital spending, unexpectedly fell in December. Excluding those for ships and electric power companies, declined by 1.2% month-on-month to 889.3 billion yen in December 2024, the worst reading in four months. The latest reading also reversed from a 3.4% growth in November and defied market expectations for a slight 0.1% gain.

On an annual basis, private-sector machinery orders increased 4.3% in December, slowing from a 10.3% surge in November and missing estimates of 6.9%.

Additionally, Japan's manufacturers' sentiment improved for the second consecutive month, with the Reuters Tankan index rising to +3 in February 2025, up from +2 in January.

The Nikkei 225 Index fell 0.2% to below 39,200 on Wednesday, ending a two-day rally as investors reacted to disappointing economic data. The Japanese yen held its recent decline to around 152 per dollar on Thursday.

More on Japan

  • Japan’s core inflation rate surges to 16-month high of 3% in December

  • Bank of Japan raises policy rate to 0.5%, highest since 2008 amid sustained inflation and rising wages

  • Japan unexpectedly logs trade surplus, exports hits record high & imports also shows positive growth

  • BOJ's Dec summary raises expectations of rate hike soon; keeps policy rate steady at 0.25%

  • Japan’s Nov headline inflation rate rises to three-month high of 2.9%; core inflation tops forecast

data.title: Bank of Cyprus targets 4% loan growth and up to 70% payout in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bank of Cyprus (OTCPK:BKCYF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Panicos Nicolaou highlighted significant achievements, including a record net profit exceeding €500M, an NPE ratio below 2%, and a 50% distribution payout for 2024 earnings. He emphasized strong shareholder value creation and raised the payout range to 50%-70% for future distributions.
  • The CEO revealed priorities for 2025, including navigating interest rate normalization, driving loan book growth, and strengthening franchise investments while maintaining strict risk and cost discipline. The bank expects high-teens ROTE on a 15% CET1 ratio in 2025.
  • CFO Eliza Livadiotou reported a 20% increase in new loans to €2.4B for 2024 and highlighted a cost-to-income ratio of 34%, despite inflationary pressures. She also noted strong liquidity and customer deposit growth of 6% year-over-year, reaching €20.5B.

Outlook

  • Management anticipates net interest income (NII) to decline below €700M in 2025, with stabilization expected in 2026 at over €650M, assuming rates remain at 2%. Loan growth is projected to continue at around 4% annually, supported by domestic demand and selective international expansion.
  • Non-NII revenue sources, including fees, commissions, and insurance income, are expected to grow at 4% annually. Insurance businesses are projected to achieve over 6% annual growth in income.
  • The bank plans to maintain a cost-to-income ratio around 40% in 2025, leveraging ongoing digital transformations to offset inflationary pressures.

Financial Results

  • Full-year 2024 profit after tax reached €508M, equivalent to earnings per share of €1.14, a 5% year-over-year increase. NII rose 4% year-over-year to €822M, despite a modest 3% quarterly decline due to lower ECB deposit rates.
  • The gross performing loan book expanded 4% year-over-year to €10.2B, driven by corporate and international lending.
  • The bank's CET1 capital ratio stood at 19.2%, with total capital at 24.0%, supported by 300 basis points of organic capital generation in 2024.
  • Operating expenses increased by 8% year-over-year, driven by inflationary pressures, IT investments, and professional fees. Cost of risk for 2024 was 30 basis points, reflecting a robust credit portfolio.

Q&A

  • Eleni Ismailou, Axia Ventures Group: Asked about deposit volume stability and capital returns. CEO Nicolaou indicated that deposit volumes and costs are expected to remain broadly flat in 2025, and the bank aims to reach the higher end of its 50%-70% payout policy soon.
  • Can Demir, Wood & Company: Queried about NPE sales, JCC income trends, and litigation provisions. CFO Livadiotou clarified that NPE sales had minimal P&L impact but were capital positive. She attributed JCC’s income trajectory to one-off costs and reaffirmed its value to the group.
  • Alfredo Alonso, Deutsche Bank: Requested clarification on capital targets and cost of risk. CEO Nicolaou reiterated high confidence in maintaining a high-teens ROTE with no evidence of asset quality deterioration.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns over NII declines, the evolving deposit mix, and litigation provisions, reflecting a slightly cautious tone. Repeated questions about excess capital indicated interest in potential shareholder returns.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing strategic achievements and future growth plans. However, responses to questions about hedging and JCC indicated measured caution.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, management’s tone remained consistent, with a focus on confidently addressing headwinds from rate normalization and market conditions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted positively, with a raised payout policy of 50%-70% for 2025, compared to the prior quarter’s 50% target. Loan growth projections remained stable at 4% annually.
  • Analysts continued to focus on NII and capital returns, with increased interest in deposit dynamics and international loan book expansion.
  • Management’s confidence in achieving high-teens ROTE remained unchanged, but discussions around hedging and litigation provisions highlighted ongoing caution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Potential NII declines due to lower rates and deposit repricing were acknowledged, with management emphasizing hedging strategies and loan growth to mitigate impacts.
  • Litigation provisions and impairments on REMU properties were noted as challenges, though management remains optimistic about ongoing de-risking efforts.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about excess capital deployment and its impact on shareholder returns, alongside questions about JCC’s strategic fit.

Final Takeaway

Bank of Cyprus showcased robust performance in 2024, achieving record profits and strengthening its capital and asset quality metrics. For 2025, the bank is focused on sustaining loan growth, diversifying revenue streams, and maintaining cost efficiency, while navigating a challenging interest rate environment. With a raised payout range and strategic growth initiatives, the bank remains committed to delivering high shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Mitsui to buy 40% stake in Australia's Rhodes Ridge iron ore project
Wix.com reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Wix.com press release (NASDAQ:WIX): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.93 beats by $0.32.
  • Revenue of $460.5M (+14.0% Y/Y) misses by $0.93M.
  • Creative Subscriptions revenue in the fourth quarter of 2024 was $329.7 million, up 11% y/y.
  • Creative Subscriptions ARR increased to $1.343 billion as of the end of the quarter, up 13% y/y.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $133.7 million, while capital expenditures totaled $2.0 million, leading to free cash flow of $131.8 million.
  • Total bookings in the fourth quarter of 2024 were $464.6 million, up 18% y/y. Total bookings on a y/y constant currency basis were $466.2 million.
  • Creative Subscriptions bookings in the fourth quarter of 2024 were $325.2 million, up 15% y/y
  • Business Solutions bookings in the fourth quarter of 2024 were $139.4 million, up 25% y/y.
  • In addition, the Company provided its initial outlook for the first quarter and full year 2025.
  • On track to achieve Rule of 45 in 2025 at high end of outlook through continued innovation-powered growth and further FCF margin expansion.

  • As such, we provide outlook for the year and the first quarter on both as-reported and constant currency bases.

    As-reported As-reportedgrowth y/y FX impact Constant currencygrowth y/y
    Full year 2025
    Bookings $2,025 - 2,060 million 11 - 13% ~$45 million 13 - 15%
    Revenue $1,970 - 2,000 million vs. consensus of $2.02B 12 - 14% ~$34 million 14 - 16%
    Free cash flow $590 - 610 million 30 - 31% margin ~$25 million 31 - 32% margin
    Q1’25
    Revenue $469 - 473 million vs. consensus of $479.78M 12 - 13% ~$6 million 13 - 14%

data.title: Fiverr International reports mixed Q4 results; initiates Q1 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Fiverr International press release (NYSE:FVRR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.64 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $103.7M (+13.3% Y/Y) beats by $2.31M.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities in the fourth quarter of 2024 was $30.0 million, compared to $27.5 million in the fourth quarter of 2023, an increase of 9.0% year over year.
  • Free cash flow1 in the fourth quarter of 2024 was $29.6 million, compared to $27.4 million in the fourth quarter of 2023, an increase of 8.1% year over year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA1 in the fourth quarter of 2024 was $20.7 million, compared to $16.1 million in the fourth quarter of 2023. Adjusted EBITDA margin1 was 20.0% in the fourth quarter of 2024, compared to 17.6% in the fourth quarter of 2023, representing a 240 basis points improvement year over year.

data.title: CVR Partners GAAP EPS of $1.73, revenue of $139.56M; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• CVR Partners press release (NYSE:UAN): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.73.
  • Revenue of $139.56M (-1.5% Y/Y).
  • Declared a fourth quarter 2024 cash distribution of $1.75 per common unit, bringing the cumulative cash distributions declared for 2024 to $6.76 per common unit.
  • Achieved a combined ammonia utilization rate of 96 percent for full-year 2024.
  • For the fourth quarter of 2024, CVR Partners’ average realized gate prices for UAN declined by 5 percent to $229 per ton and ammonia improved by 3 percent to $475 per ton when compared to the fourth quarter of 2023. Average realized gate prices for UAN and ammonia were $241 per ton and $461 per ton, respectively, for the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • EBITDA of $50 million.
  • “Looking forward, we will continue to focus on the high utilization of our plants and the generation of free cash flow,” Pytosh said. “In addition, CVR Partners is pleased to declare a fourth quarter 2024 cash distribution of $1.75 per common unit.”

  • Q1 2025 Outlook

    The table below summarizes our outlook for certain operational statistics and financial information for the first quarter of 2025. See “Forward-Looking Statements” above.

    Low High
    Ammonia utilization rate 95 % 100 %

data.title: HSBC Non-GAAP EPS of $1.31, revenue of $65.9B; initiates FY25 and updates long-term outlook
data.description:
• HSBC Holdings press release (NYSE:HSBC): FY Non-GAAP Basic EPS of $1.31.
  • Gaap Diluted earnings per share of $1.24.
  • Revenue of $65.9B (-0.3% Y/Y) misses by $720M. Constant currency revenue excluding notable items rose by $2.9B to $67.4B.
  • Profit after tax increased by $0.4bn to $25.0B.
  • Constant currency profit before tax excluding notable items increased by $1.4B to $34.1B.
  • Net interest income decreased by $3.1B.
  • Net interest margin of 1.56% decreased by 10 basis points.
  • Expected credit losses and other credit impairment charges of $3.4B were stable.
  • Customer lending balances fell by $8B on a reported basis but rose by $14B on a constant currency basis.
  • Customer accounts rose by $43bn on a reported basis, and $75bn on a constant currency basis.
  • Common equity tier 1 capital ratio of 14.9% rose by 0.1 of a percentage point.
  • The Board has approved a fourth interim dividend of $0.36 per share, resulting in a total of $0.87 per share in respect of 2024, inclusive of a special dividend of $0.21 per share.
  • The bank also intends to initiate a share buy-back of up to $2bn, which we expect to complete by our first quarter 2025 results announcement.
  • Outlook – "We are now targeting a mid-teens return on average tangible equity in each of the three years from 2025 to 2027 excluding notable items, while acknowledging the outlook for interest rates remains volatile and uncertain, particularly in the medium term."
  • "We expect banking NII of around $42bn in 2025. Our current expectation reflects modelling of a number of market-dependent factors. If changes in these factors impact the output of our modelling, we would update our expectation for 2025 Banking NII in future quarterly results announcements."
  • "We retain a Group-wide focus on cost discipline. We are targeting growth in target basis operating expenses of approximately 3% in 2025 compared with 2024. Our cost target includes the impact of simplification-related saves associated with our announced reorganisation, which aims to generate approximately $0.3bn of cost reductions in 2025, with a commitment to an annualised reduction of $1.5bn in our cost base expected by the end of 2026. To deliver these reductions, we plan to incur severance and other up-front costs of $1.8bn over 2025 and 2026, which will be classified as notable items."
  • "We expect ECL charges as a percentage of average gross loans to continue to be within our medium-term planning range of 30bps to 40bps in 2025 (including lending held for sale balances). "
  • "Over the medium to long term, we continue to expect mid-single digit percentage growth for year-on-year customer lending balances. We expect double-digit percentage average annual growth in fee and other income in Wealth over the medium-term."
  • "We intend to continue to manage the CET1 capital ratio within our medium-term target range of 14% to 14.5%, with a dividend payout ratio target basis of 50% for 2025, excluding material notable items and related impacts."

data.title: Hackett Group targets $75M-$76.5M Q1 revenue amid Gen AI growth, SAP strength
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Hackett Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:HCKT) Q4 2024

Management View

  • The Hackett Group reported total revenues of $79.2 million and adjusted earnings per share of $0.47, exceeding quarterly guidance. Strong performance was driven by the overperformance of the SAP segment and increased revenue growth from the Global S&BT (GSBT) segment, particularly from Gen AI engagements.
  • CEO Ted Fernandez highlighted the significant contributions of the company's AI XPLR and ZBrain platforms, stating that these advancements enable Hackett to provide clients with fully integrated AI solutions. He emphasized that "AI XPLR's capability to dynamically ideate and evaluate Agentic Gen AI solutions" is highly unique.
  • The SAP Solutions segment achieved its fourth consecutive quarter of overperformance, bolstered by expanded sales efforts and increased value-added reseller transactions. The GSBT segment saw a 4% revenue increase, partially offset by weakness in the eProcurement practice.
  • CFO Rob Ramirez reported adjusted gross margins of 47.7%, an improvement from 43.3% in the prior year, driven by high-margin Gen AI engagements and reseller activity. Adjusted diluted earnings per share of $0.47 marked an increase from $0.39 in the prior year.
  • The company repurchased 117,000 shares at an average of $30.95 per share and announced a 9% dividend increase to $0.48 annually, with a declared Q1 dividend of $0.12 per share.

Outlook

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 is projected to be in the range of $75 million to $76.5 million. Adjusted diluted EPS is expected to range between $0.39 and $0.41, with an adjusted gross margin of approximately 43% to 44%.
  • The GSBT segment is expected to grow 5% to 10% year-over-year in Q1, driven by Gen AI growth, while Oracle and SAP Solutions are anticipated to decline 8% to 10% combined. Management projected strong demand for SAP services throughout 2025.
  • Management noted that IT budgets for Gen AI initiatives are expected to increase significantly in 2025, transitioning from awareness to budgeted projects.

Financial Results

  • Total revenues were $79.2 million, with revenues before reimbursements at $77.5 million, surpassing high-end guidance. Adjusted EBITDA totaled $19.5 million, representing 25.2% of revenues before reimbursements.
  • The SAP Solutions segment grew 51% year-over-year, reaching $16.8 million in revenues before reimbursements, primarily due to software-related sales resulting from expanded sales investments.
  • The GSBT segment reported revenues of $43.9 million, with strong growth in Gen AI consulting offset by declines in eProcurement and OneStream implementations. The Oracle Solutions segment saw a 6% year-over-year revenue decline to $17.4 million, impacted by the wind-down of a large post-go-live engagement.
  • Cash balances increased to $16.4 million, supported by $20.6 million in net cash from operating activities. The company reduced its total debt to $13 million and maintained a stock repurchase authorization of $27.5 million.

Q&A

  • George Sutton, Craig-Hallum: Asked about Gen AI pipeline and its conversion to implementation contracts. CEO Fernandez emphasized that clients are now budgeting for 2025 initiatives, highlighting the company's preparedness and unique capabilities.
  • Jeff Martin, ROTH Capital Partners: Inquired about scaling Gen AI implementation capabilities. Fernandez stated the company's goal to double its Gen AI resources as quickly as possible.
  • Vincent Colicchio, Barrington Research: Questioned the long-term outlook for Oracle and eProcurement. Fernandez noted Oracle activity remains strong, while eProcurement headwinds are expected to diminish by Q2 2025.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed optimism about the company's Gen AI positioning but raised concerns about declines in Oracle and eProcurement segments. Questions reflected a cautious yet curious approach to the scaling of AI-driven capabilities.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, describing their offerings as "highly differentiated" and emphasizing the transformative potential of Gen AI. CEO Fernandez frequently highlighted the company's unique positioning and preparedness for 2025.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts appeared more focused on Gen AI scalability and the impact on broader revenue streams. Management's tone remained consistent, reinforcing confidence in growth opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted EPS increased to $0.47 from $0.43 in Q3 2024, driven by higher-margin Gen AI engagements and SAP reseller activity.
  • Revenues before reimbursements for the SAP segment grew from $12.9 million in Q3 to $16.8 million in Q4, reflecting a 51% year-over-year increase.
  • GSBT segment revenue growth accelerated to 4% in Q4, compared to flat year-over-year performance in Q3, highlighting improved traction in Gen AI initiatives.
  • Management's confidence in Gen AI-driven opportunities remained strong, though they acknowledged challenges in Oracle and eProcurement practices.

Risks and Concerns

  • Continued weakness in eProcurement and OneStream practices could weigh on GSBT segment growth. Management noted that these issues are expected to stabilize by Q2 2025.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the scalability of Gen AI implementation capabilities to meet rising demand. Management plans to address this through aggressive resource expansion.
  • The wind-down of a large Oracle engagement may impact near-term revenue growth in that segment.

Final Takeaway

The Hackett Group's Q4 results showcased robust growth in Gen AI and SAP-driven revenues, supported by strategic investments and acquisitions. Management's emphasis on scaling Gen AI capabilities and leveraging proprietary platforms positions the company to capitalize on increasing IT budgets in 2025. Despite challenges in Oracle and eProcurement practices, the company remains confident in its ability to drive long-term value creation through innovative solutions and targeted resource expansion.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
